channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Teach For All Media and Editorial Coordinator (Remote): Global Communications Job Opportunity Across 35+ Countries
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Teach For All is inviting applications for the position of Media and Editorial Coordinator, offering an exciting opportunity for communications and media professionals who are passionate about storytelling, global education, and social impact. This fully remote position is open to applicants who are legally authorized to work in more than 35 countries across Africa, Asia, […]

The post Teach For All Media and Editorial Coordinator (Remote): Global Communications Job Opportunity Across 35+ Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Teach For All is inviting applications for the position of Media and Editorial Coordinator, offering an exciting opportunity for communications and media professionals who are passionate about storytelling, global education, and social impact. This fully remote position is open to applicants who are legally authorized to work in more than 35 countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Oceania.

As Teach For All continues advancing its 25-year vision of ensuring that every child has access to quality education and the opportunity to reach their full potential, the organization is seeking a highly organized and creative professional to help amplify the voices, stories, and insights emerging from its global network.

This role is ideal for professionals with experience in journalism, media relations, communications, editorial management, or public engagement who want to contribute to meaningful global change while working in an international, mission-driven environment.

Application Status: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

About Teach For All

Teach For All is a global network of more than 60 independent, locally led organizations working together to expand educational opportunity worldwide.

The organization partners with local education leaders, teachers, governments, and communities to develop collective leadership that enables children from all backgrounds to thrive.

Rather than operating schools directly, Teach For All supports its network partners through global learning, coaching, consulting, collaboration, and access to international resources that strengthen education systems around the world.

About the Media and Public Engagement Team

The Media and Public Engagement team helps shape global conversations around education by positioning Teach For All as a leading voice in educational equity and systems change.

The team works proactively to:

  • Build compelling narratives around Teach For All’s mission.
  • Share impactful stories from network partners.
  • Engage global media outlets and journalists.
  • Publish thought leadership content.
  • Increase public awareness of education challenges and solutions.
  • Support strategic communications across the organization’s global network.

The Media and Editorial Coordinator plays a central role in ensuring these efforts run efficiently while maintaining high editorial standards.

Position Summary

The Media and Editorial Coordinator will support media relations, editorial planning, thought leadership, and operational systems that enable Teach For All to communicate effectively with global audiences.

The role combines strategic communication with hands-on project management, making it suitable for professionals who enjoy balancing creativity with operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

1. Media Relations (30%)

Successful candidates will help strengthen Teach For All’s relationships with journalists and media organizations by:

  • Supporting media engagement activities.
  • Managing relationships with journalists and editors.
  • Conducting media landscape research.
  • Identifying relevant media platforms for outreach.
  • Maintaining media contact databases.
  • Managing incoming media inquiries.
  • Monitoring daily media coverage.
  • Tracking Teach For All’s media presence.
  • Identifying new engagement opportunities.
  • Analyzing media coverage and producing actionable insights.

2. Thought Leadership and Public Engagement (25%)

The successful candidate will contribute to developing high-quality thought leadership content by:

  • Supporting the creation of opinion articles (op-eds).
  • Assisting with essays and expert commentary.
  • Conducting background research.
  • Drafting and editing written materials.
  • Coordinating editorial submissions.
  • Managing editorial calendars and publication workflows.
  • Maintaining consistent editorial quality across content.
  • Supporting written and video public engagement initiatives.
  • Coordinating speaking opportunities.
  • Preparing press releases and media kits.

3. Operations and Systems (45%)

Operational excellence is a major component of this position.

Responsibilities include:

  • Managing spokesperson databases.
  • Coordinating spokesperson preparation.
  • Maintaining journalist CRM systems.
  • Managing media monitoring systems.
  • Tracking editorial output.
  • Monitoring media placements.
  • Maintaining workflow trackers.
  • Supporting collaboration across internal teams.
  • Coordinating event logistics.
  • Scheduling meetings.
  • Preparing briefing documents.
  • Managing the team’s media inquiry inbox.
  • Supporting post-event follow-up activities.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for professionals working in:

  • Journalism
  • Media Relations
  • Communications
  • Public Relations
  • Editorial Management
  • Content Strategy
  • Corporate Communications
  • International Development
  • Education
  • Non-profit Communications
  • Social Impact Organizations

Candidates who enjoy storytelling, strategic communication, and global collaboration will be particularly well suited for this role.

Required Skills and Qualifications

Teach For All is looking for candidates with:

  • Excellent writing and storytelling skills.
  • Strong editing and proofreading abilities.
  • A solid understanding of media relations.
  • Knowledge of how newsrooms operate.
  • Strong organizational and project management skills.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
  • Analytical skills for media monitoring and reporting.
  • Ability to work across cultures and time zones.
  • A proactive, solutions-oriented mindset.
  • Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and continuous learning.

Preferred Experience

While not mandatory, candidates with the following experience will be highly competitive:

  • Three or more years of professional experience in journalism, communications, media relations, think tanks, or related fields.
  • Experience working within education, international development, or social impact organizations.
  • Familiarity with media monitoring platforms.
  • Experience using Salesforce or similar CRM systems.
  • Experience supporting spokesperson preparation or media training.

What Teach For All Offers

Successful candidates will benefit from:

  • Fully remote work.
  • A competitive salary based on country of employment and experience.
  • Comprehensive employee benefits.
  • Flexible working arrangements.
  • Generous paid time off.
  • Professional growth opportunities.
  • International collaboration.
  • Meaningful work with global impact.
  • Opportunities for long-term career progression beyond the initial one-year contract.

Employment Terms

Successful candidates should note:

  • Initial contract duration is one year.
  • Strong potential for extension and career growth.
  • Up to 20% travel may be required.
  • Some meetings will occur outside traditional working hours due to global time zones.
  • Visa sponsorship is not available.
  • Applicants must already possess legal work authorization in their country of hire.

Eligible Countries

Applications are accepted from candidates legally authorized to work in the following locations:

  • Australia
  • Bangladesh
  • Belgium
  • Canada
  • Chile
  • Colombia
  • Côte d’Ivoire
  • Dominican Republic
  • Ecuador
  • Ghana
  • Hong Kong
  • India
  • Indonesia
  • Italy
  • Jordan
  • Kenya
  • Malawi
  • Malaysia
  • Mauritius
  • Mexico
  • Morocco
  • New Zealand
  • Nigeria
  • Pakistan
  • Peru
  • Philippines
  • Puerto Rico
  • Qatar
  • Rwanda
  • Singapore
  • South Africa
  • Spain
  • Thailand
  • Uganda
  • Zambia

How to Apply

Applicants should prepare:

  • An up-to-date Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • A one-page motivation letter written in English explaining:
    • Why they are interested in the role.
    • How their experience aligns with the position.
    • What they hope to contribute to Teach For All’s mission.

Click here for more and to apply

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

For communications professionals seeking a globally impactful career, this role offers the rare opportunity to influence international conversations around education while collaborating with experts and organizations across more than 60 countries.

Whether your passion lies in journalism, strategic communications, editorial excellence, or public engagement, joining Teach For All means becoming part of a mission-driven organization working to expand educational opportunity for children around the world.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post Teach For All Media and Editorial Coordinator (Remote): Global Communications Job Opportunity Across 35+ Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Greenpeace MENA Remote Engagement Director Job Opportunity (2026): Strategic Leadership Role in Environmental Advocacy Across the MENA Region
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The environmental advocacy space continues to expand rapidly, and organizations are increasingly seeking experienced leaders who can drive meaningful engagement, strengthen supporter communities, and build powerful communication strategies. One such opportunity is the Remote Engagement Director position at Greenpeace MENA, a senior leadership role designed for professionals passionate about environmental change, strategic communication, and audience […]

The post Greenpeace MENA Remote Engagement Director Job Opportunity (2026): Strategic Leadership Role in Environmental Advocacy Across the MENA Region appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The environmental advocacy space continues to expand rapidly, and organizations are increasingly seeking experienced leaders who can drive meaningful engagement, strengthen supporter communities, and build powerful communication strategies. One such opportunity is the Remote Engagement Director position at Greenpeace MENA, a senior leadership role designed for professionals passionate about environmental change, strategic communication, and audience mobilization.

This position plays a critical role in shaping how Greenpeace connects with supporters, builds campaigns, and strengthens its influence across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

The application deadline for this opportunity is 23 July 2026.

Overview of the Engagement Director Role

The Engagement Director is a senior-level leadership position within the GP MENA Engagement Department. The role reports directly to the Executive Director and operates as part of the Senior Management Team.

This position is responsible for designing, managing, and refining engagement strategies that support Greenpeace’s mission, including communication, digital outreach, supporter engagement, and fundraising integration.

The role is fully remote but focused on the MENA region, requiring strong cross-cultural leadership and collaboration across distributed teams.

Key Responsibilities

1. Strategic Direction and Program Leadership

The Engagement Director is responsible for shaping the overall engagement strategy, ensuring alignment with Greenpeace MENA’s mission and long-term goals.

Key duties include:

  • Designing integrated engagement strategies combining:
    • Communications
    • Digital campaigns
    • Media outreach
    • Community engagement initiatives
  • Defining measurable KPIs for campaigns and fundraising success
  • Leading strategic storytelling efforts to strengthen Greenpeace’s brand narrative
  • Developing audience growth strategies to expand supporter bases across MENA
  • Building strong media and stakeholder relationships across traditional and digital platforms
  • Staying updated with trends in digital communications, advocacy tools, and NGO innovations
  • Overseeing engagement budgets, resource allocation, and financial planning
  • Aligning engagement strategies with fundraising goals for maximum impact

2. Leadership, Team Management, and Coaching

This role includes direct responsibility for leading and developing the engagement team.

Responsibilities include:

  • Managing staff performance and setting clear objectives
  • Providing coaching, mentoring, and professional development support
  • Ensuring high-quality outputs through performance monitoring and feedback
  • Coordinating with Programme and Fundraising Directors to maximize campaign impact
  • Acting as a key representative for engagement across Greenpeace International networks
  • Supporting global collaboration and shared campaign strategies

3. Audience Development and Supporter Engagement

A major focus of the role is building strong, long-term relationships with supporters.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Designing and optimizing supporter journeys for new and existing audiences
  • Enhancing supporter retention and loyalty through targeted engagement strategies
  • Collaborating with fundraising teams to strengthen donor relationships
  • Managing data systems for audience segmentation and performance tracking
  • Improving supporter care through effective communication strategies
  • Ensuring storytelling is embedded in all engagement campaigns

4. Data, Strategy, and Performance Management

The role requires strong analytical and strategic planning capabilities.

Key expectations include:

  • Using data-driven insights to guide engagement decisions
  • Developing forecasting models for income and engagement performance
  • Managing reporting systems for internal and external stakeholders
  • Improving digital engagement platforms and processes
  • Ensuring effective measurement of campaign impact

5. External Partnerships and Network Building

The Engagement Director will also:

  • Build relationships with external partners, agencies, and suppliers
  • Strengthen collaborations across media and advocacy networks
  • Represent Greenpeace MENA in global Greenpeace initiatives
  • Develop partnerships that amplify engagement reach and effectiveness

Required Qualifications and Experience

Educational Background

  • Bachelor’s degree in:
    • Marketing
    • Communications
    • Journalism
    • Advertising
    • Media Studies, or a related field

Professional Experience

  • Minimum of 10+ years of experience in engagement, communications, or advocacy roles
  • Senior-level experience in NGO or international organization environments
  • Proven leadership of teams and large-scale engagement programs
  • Strong background in digital and offline audience mobilization

Core Skills

  • Expertise in engagement strategy and supporter acquisition
  • Strong digital communications and marketing experience
  • Proven data analysis and reporting capabilities
  • Experience in fundraising or donor engagement strategies
  • Ability to design supporter journeys and segmentation models

Technical and Functional Competencies

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Excellent English communication skills (Arabic is an advantage)
  • Strong leadership and team development abilities
  • Deep understanding of NGO operations and advocacy environments
  • Ability to manage budgets and forecast performance
  • Experience with media strategy and public engagement campaigns
  • Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills
  • Ability to work effectively in multicultural and remote teams

Leadership Expectations

The ideal candidate should be able to:

  • Develop and execute long-term engagement strategies
  • Lead cross-functional teams across multiple regions
  • Act as a strategic advisor in campaign planning
  • Empower teams and foster innovation in communication approaches
  • Strengthen audience-centered organizational culture
  • Influence internal and external stakeholders effectively

Commitment and Organizational Values

Greenpeace MENA operates under the broader mission of Greenpeace International, promoting environmental protection through non-violent advocacy and direct action campaigns.

The organization is committed to:

  • Environmental justice
  • Peaceful activism
  • Diversity and inclusion in hiring practices
  • Equal opportunity employment regardless of background

Application Process

Interested candidates are required to submit:

  • Updated CV
  • Cover Letter

Click here for more and to apply

Deadline: 23 July 2026

Conclusion

This Engagement Director role represents a high-impact leadership opportunity for professionals who are passionate about environmental advocacy, audience engagement, and strategic communication. It is a chance to shape regional campaigns, influence global conversations, and drive meaningful environmental action across the MENA region.

Click here for more global job opportunities

 

The post Greenpeace MENA Remote Engagement Director Job Opportunity (2026): Strategic Leadership Role in Environmental Advocacy Across the MENA Region appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security Fellowship (PIBBSS) Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 in South Africa: Receive $3,000 Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation, Flights, Mentorship, and Global Artificial Intelligence Safety Research Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications are now open for the highly competitive Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security (PIBBSS) Winter Fellowship 2026–2027, an interdisciplinary Artificial Intelligence (AI) safety research fellowship designed for outstanding researchers from around the world. Hosted by Principles of Intelligence, the fellowship offers researchers a unique opportunity to transition into the rapidly growing […]

The post Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security Fellowship (PIBBSS) Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 in South Africa: Receive $3,000 Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation, Flights, Mentorship, and Global Artificial Intelligence Safety Research Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the highly competitive Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security (PIBBSS) Winter Fellowship 2026–2027, an interdisciplinary Artificial Intelligence (AI) safety research fellowship designed for outstanding researchers from around the world.

Hosted by Principles of Intelligence, the fellowship offers researchers a unique opportunity to transition into the rapidly growing field of AI safety while working alongside leading mentors, scholars, and practitioners. The program is specifically designed to connect talented researchers from diverse academic backgrounds with experienced AI safety mentors and research communities.

The fellowship will run for approximately three months, from November 2026 to February 2027, primarily in Cape Town, South Africa, with remote participation available only for exceptional candidates.

Participants will receive generous financial support, dedicated mentorship, accommodation, travel support, and access to a vibrant interdisciplinary community focused on addressing some of the world’s most important AI-related challenges.

About the PIBBSS Fellowship

The Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security Fellowship is an intensive research program focused on advancing AI safety through interdisciplinary collaboration.

The program recognizes that many of the most important questions surrounding advanced AI systems cannot be solved by computer science alone. Instead, meaningful progress requires contributions from researchers across numerous disciplines that study intelligence, complex systems, governance, ethics, cognition, and human behavior.

The fellowship therefore brings together researchers from different academic and professional backgrounds and supports them in developing impactful AI safety research projects under expert guidance.

Over the years, fellowship alumni have gone on to work in:

  • AI safety laboratories
  • Government institutions
  • Universities
  • Independent research organizations
  • AI governance initiatives
  • Technology policy organizations

Past fellows have secured positions at leading institutions including Harvard University, the University of Oxford, Anthropic, Google, Epoch, and various AI safety organizations.

Fellowship Duration and Location

Successful applicants will participate in:

  • A pre-program AI risk reading group conducted remotely
  • A multi-day opening retreat
  • Three months of full-time mentored research
  • Community events and speaker sessions
  • A final symposium presentation in Spring 2027
  • A closing retreat with fellow researchers and mentors

Program Timeline

  • Fellowship Period: November 2026 – February 2027
  • Duration: Approximately 3 months
  • Location: Cape Town, South Africa
  • Participation Mode: In-person (remote participation only for exceptional candidates)

Benefits of the PIBBSS Winter Fellowship 2026–2027

The fellowship provides substantial support to ensure participants can fully focus on their research.

Financial Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

  • Monthly stipend of $3,000
  • Funding for approximately three months
  • One return flight to Cape Town, South Africa
  • Visa support letters where required

Accommodation and Living Support

Participants will also receive:

  • Free accommodation
  • Private bedroom for each fellow
  • Shared apartment arrangements where applicable
  • Daily workday meals
  • Lunch, dinner, and snacks provided at the office

Academic and Professional Benefits

Fellows will gain access to:

  • Dedicated AI safety mentors
  • Personalized project development support
  • Research strategy coaching
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities
  • Regular speaker talks
  • Professional networking events
  • Shared office space
  • Final symposium presentation opportunities

Career Development Benefits

The fellowship is particularly valuable for researchers seeking to:

  • Transition into AI safety research
  • Build international research networks
  • Develop publishable research outputs
  • Engage with leading AI safety experts
  • Explore AI governance and policy pathways
  • Access future career opportunities in academia, industry, and public policy

Research Areas and Fields Encouraged to Apply

The fellowship actively seeks researchers from diverse disciplines that contribute insights into intelligence, decision-making, governance, and complex systems.

Relevant fields include:

  1. Mathematics
  2. Neuroscience
  3. Cognitive Science
  4. Dynamical Systems Theory
  5. Physics
  6. Philosophy
  7. Political Theory
  8. Economic Theory
  9. Ecology
  10. Evolutionary Biology
  11. Linguistics
  12. Media Studies
  13. Humanities
  14. Related research disciplines

Researchers from interdisciplinary backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

Who Can Apply?

The fellowship is intended for individuals who are motivated to contribute to AI safety and possess research experience in fields related to complex and intelligent systems.

Applicants may include:

  • PhD researchers
  • Doctoral candidates
  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • Academic researchers
  • Independent researchers
  • Experienced professionals with significant research backgrounds

Importantly, applicants do not need to hold a specific academic credential if they can demonstrate substantial research experience.

The fellowship welcomes applicants from all countries worldwide.

Another major advantage is that applicants are not required to have a fully developed research project before applying. The fellowship team works closely with selected candidates during the interview process to identify suitable mentors and refine project ideas.

Fellowship Structure

The fellowship follows a carefully designed progression that supports fellows throughout the research journey.

Pre-Program Phase

Participants begin with:

  • Remote reading groups
  • AI risk discussions
  • Foundational AI safety materials
  • Community-building activities

Research Phase

During the core fellowship period, fellows engage in:

  • Full-time research
  • Regular mentorship meetings
  • Research coaching sessions
  • Collaborative discussions
  • Community events

Final Presentation Phase

The program concludes with:

  • Research presentations
  • Symposium participation
  • Networking opportunities
  • Knowledge-sharing sessions

Application Process

The selection process is conducted in multiple stages.

Stage 1: Written Application

Applicants must submit:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV) or résumé
  • Personal statement (600–800 words)
  • Research background information
  • Motivation for pursuing AI safety research
  • Optional but recommended work samples

Stages 2–4: Interviews and Project Development

Shortlisted candidates will participate in:

  • Multiple interview rounds
  • Research interest discussions
  • Project proposal development
  • Mentor matching sessions

The selection process is designed to identify talented researchers while ensuring strong alignment between fellows and mentors.

Why the PIBBSS Fellowship Stands Out

Unlike many traditional research fellowships, the PIBBSS Fellowship emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration and mentorship.

Participants join a global network of researchers who are exploring:

  • AI alignment
  • AI governance
  • AI ethics
  • Cognitive science and AI intersections
  • Policy responses to emerging AI risks
  • Human and machine intelligence comparisons
  • Long-term AI safety challenges

This collaborative environment enables fellows to contribute fresh perspectives and innovative approaches to some of the most pressing issues in artificial intelligence.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the PIBBSS Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 is:

20 July 2026 (23:59 Anywhere on Earth).

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible to allow sufficient time for review and interview scheduling.

Researchers interested in advancing AI safety while working alongside world-class mentors in an international environment should strongly consider applying for this fully funded fellowship opportunity.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security Fellowship (PIBBSS) Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 in South Africa: Receive $3,000 Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation, Flights, Mentorship, and Global Artificial Intelligence Safety Research Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Canada’s Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator 2026: Expand Your Fintech Business into Latin America’s Fast-Growing Market
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Canadian fintech companies looking to expand internationally now have an exceptional opportunity to enter one of Latin America’s fastest-growing digital finance markets through the Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator (CTA) 2026. Delivered by the Trade Commissioner Service (TCS) under the Government of Canada’s Canadian Technology Accelerators (CTA) initiative, this 13-week hybrid business development program is […]

The post Canada’s Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator 2026: Expand Your Fintech Business into Latin America’s Fast-Growing Market appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Canadian fintech companies looking to expand internationally now have an exceptional opportunity to enter one of Latin America’s fastest-growing digital finance markets through the Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator (CTA) 2026.

Delivered by the Trade Commissioner Service (TCS) under the Government of Canada’s Canadian Technology Accelerators (CTA) initiative, this 13-week hybrid business development program is designed to help innovative Canadian fintech companies successfully explore, enter, and grow within Mexico’s rapidly expanding financial technology ecosystem.

With tailored mentorship, business matchmaking, investor introductions, and an immersive in-market experience in Mexico City, the program equips participants with the knowledge, partnerships, and market intelligence needed for successful international expansion.

Application Deadline

15 July 2026

 

About the Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator

Mexico has become one of the most attractive fintech destinations in Latin America.

Several factors have contributed to this momentum, including:

  • Rapid growth of the fintech industry
  • A large underbanked and underserved population
  • Progressive fintech regulations
  • Increasing digital financial adoption
  • Strong investor interest
  • Growing demand for innovative financial technologies

Mexico was the first country in Latin America to introduce a comprehensive fintech law, creating an environment that encourages innovation while welcoming foreign fintech companies.

Today, Mexico is the second-largest fintech ecosystem in Latin America, growing at approximately 20% annually and projected to surpass 86 million digital financial service users by the end of 2027.

These market dynamics create tremendous opportunities for Canadian fintech innovators specializing in:

  • Digital payments
  • Credit solutions
  • Financial infrastructure
  • Enterprise fintech
  • Financial inclusion
  • Cybersecurity
  • Emerging financial technologies

The Canadian Technology Accelerator helps participating companies capitalize on these opportunities through structured business development support.

 

Program Dates

  • Program Duration: 26 August – 20 November 2026
  • Application Deadline: 15 July 2026
  • Weekly Commitment: Approximately 2.5 hours

The accelerator follows a hybrid delivery model that combines virtual programming with an in-person business development mission to Mexico City.

 

Why Join the Mexico Fintech CTA?

Participating companies receive practical support designed specifically for international business expansion.

The accelerator goes beyond traditional training by facilitating direct engagement with potential customers, investors, regulators, and strategic partners.

 

Program Benefits

Selected companies will gain access to numerous advantages, including:

Comprehensive Market Intelligence

Participants receive an in-depth briefing covering:

  • Mexican fintech regulations
  • Business culture
  • Market opportunities
  • Import considerations
  • Commercial best practices
  • Market entry strategies

 

Targeted Business-to-Business Meetings

Companies will participate in carefully curated meetings with:

  • Government officials
  • Industry experts
  • Venture capital firms
  • Business chambers
  • Financial institutions
  • Regulators
  • Potential clients
  • Potential distributors
  • Strategic partners

 

Personalized Mentorship

Every company benefits from tailored mentoring designed to help them:

  • Refine market entry strategies
  • Improve investor pitches
  • Strengthen business positioning
  • Develop partnerships
  • Navigate Mexico’s fintech ecosystem

 

Expert Workshops

Participants will join workshops covering topics such as:

  • Doing business in Mexico
  • Mexican fintech regulations
  • Investment opportunities
  • Industry trends
  • Market expansion strategies

 

Investor and Buyer Exposure

The accelerator creates opportunities to meet:

  • Investors
  • Venture capital firms
  • Potential buyers
  • Corporate partners
  • Strategic collaborators

 

Pitching Opportunities

Throughout the program, companies will have multiple opportunities to present their innovations directly to key decision-makers in Mexico.

 

Who Should Apply?

The accelerator seeks innovative Canadian fintech companies offering differentiated solutions in areas including:

  • Digital payments
  • Cross-border payment technologies
  • Alternative lending
  • Financial inclusion
  • Fraud prevention
  • Cybersecurity
  • Payroll platforms
  • Human resource technologies
  • Employee compensation platforms

 

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, applicants must demonstrate the following:

Validated Technology

Your company should offer an innovative and differentiated product, technology, or service.

 

Proven Market Fit

Applicants should have:

  • An established customer base
  • Product-market validation
  • Clearly identified target users

 

Business Traction

Evidence of company maturity may include:

  • Revenue generation
  • Investment received
  • A minimum viable product (MVP)
  • Customer adoption
  • Other indicators of commercial success

 

Strong Leadership

Companies should have an experienced management team with at least one senior executive able to participate throughout the entire accelerator.

 

Capacity for International Expansion

Applicants must possess sufficient financial and organizational resources to successfully pursue growth in Mexico.

 

Program Structure

The 13-week accelerator consists of four phases.

Phase 1: Market Preparedness

26 August – 25 September 2026

Activities include:

  • Individual introductory meetings
  • Onboarding session
  • Webinar: How to Do Business in Mexico
  • Webinar: Mexican Fintech Ecosystem
  • Initial mentoring sessions

 

Phase 2: Business Development and Matchmaking

28 September – 6 November 2026

During this phase, companies receive:

  • Tailored B2B meetings
  • Business matchmaking
  • Client introductions
  • Distributor introductions
  • Ongoing mentorship

 

Phase 3: In-Market Business Mission

Mexico City | 9–13 November 2026

Participants travel to Mexico City for an intensive week of business engagement featuring:

  • Embassy plenary sessions
  • Curated B2B meetings
  • Follow-up meetings
  • Site visits
  • Expert roundtables
  • Networking receptions
  • Pitch sessions
  • Cohort activities
  • Final mentoring sessions

 

Phase 4: Post-Program Support

16–20 November 2026

The accelerator concludes with:

  • Follow-up meetings
  • Remaining mentorship sessions
  • Offboarding session
  • Business continuity planning

 

Why Mexico?

Mexico continues to emerge as one of the world’s most promising fintech markets.

Its rapidly expanding digital economy, supportive regulatory framework, increasing smartphone adoption, and growing demand for financial innovation make it an ideal destination for Canadian fintech companies seeking international expansion.

The country offers opportunities across virtually every fintech segment while providing direct access to Latin America’s broader financial ecosystem.

 

Why Participate?

The Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator provides much more than business training.

Participants receive personalized support from experienced trade professionals while building relationships with investors, customers, regulators, and strategic partners that can accelerate international growth.

For Canadian fintech companies ready to scale beyond domestic markets, this accelerator represents an outstanding opportunity to establish a lasting presence in one of Latin America’s most dynamic financial technology ecosystems.

 

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post Canada’s Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator 2026: Expand Your Fintech Business into Latin America’s Fast-Growing Market appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Yiaga Africa Regional Community Organizing Institute 2026 for Young Democracy and Development Leaders in West Africa (Fully Funded)
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Young leaders and community organizers across West Africa now have an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their leadership skills, expand their regional networks, and deepen their impact in advancing democracy, development, and the rule of law through the Yiaga Africa Regional Community Organizing Institute 2026. Hosted by Yiaga Africa with support from the National Endowment for […]

The post Yiaga Africa Regional Community Organizing Institute 2026 for Young Democracy and Development Leaders in West Africa (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders and community organizers across West Africa now have an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their leadership skills, expand their regional networks, and deepen their impact in advancing democracy, development, and the rule of law through the Yiaga Africa Regional Community Organizing Institute 2026.

Hosted by Yiaga Africa with support from the National Endowment for Democracy (NED), the Regional Community Organizing Institute (COI) is a prestigious training and capacity development program designed to equip emerging and mid-career leaders with the practical tools needed to drive social change, strengthen democratic institutions, and build resilient communities across West Africa.

Formerly known as the Youth Organizing School, the Institute has evolved into one of the region’s leading platforms for developing community organizers, civic leaders, policy advocates, and movement builders who are committed to promoting democratic values and sustainable development.

Application Deadline

Deadline: July 14, 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the deadline due to the high volume of applications received annually.

About the Regional Community Organizing Institute

The West Africa Regional Community Organizing Institute was established in response to the growing demand for practical leadership development opportunities for civic activists and community organizers working in environments where democracy, freedom, human development, and good governance face significant challenges.

The program serves as a bridge connecting organizers and change-makers from both Anglophone and Francophone West Africa, creating opportunities for collaboration, knowledge exchange, and regional solidarity.

Each year, approximately 30 to 35 outstanding participants are selected from hundreds of applications submitted across the region. Participants are chosen based on their demonstrated contributions to society and their potential to create even greater impact through enhanced leadership and organizing skills.

Over the years, the Institute has built an impressive network of more than 400 alumni from countries including:

  • Cameroon
  • The Gambia
  • Ghana
  • Ivory Coast
  • Liberia
  • Nigeria
  • Sierra Leone

These alumni continue to serve as influential leaders in civil society organizations, grassroots movements, advocacy campaigns, youth organizations, and democratic governance initiatives across the region.

Why the Program Matters

Across West Africa, many communities face challenges related to governance, civic participation, accountability, social justice, and development. Addressing these challenges requires skilled leaders who can mobilize communities, build coalitions, advocate for policy reforms, and inspire collective action.

The Regional Community Organizing Institute helps participants develop these critical skills by providing practical training rooted in real-world experience and community engagement.

The program particularly seeks to strengthen the capacity of young leaders and women-led organizations while fostering cross-border collaboration among civic actors throughout the region.

Benefits of Participating in the Program

Participants selected for the Regional Community Organizing Institute will benefit from:

1. Fully Funded Participation

Yiaga Africa will cover all costs associated with participation in the Institute, removing financial barriers for selected participants.

2. Advanced Leadership Development

Participants will receive practical leadership training focused on community organizing, movement building, advocacy, and democratic engagement.

3. Regional Networking Opportunities

The program provides a unique platform to connect with civic leaders, activists, organizers, and development practitioners from across West Africa.

4. Access to Expert Faculty

Participants will learn from an interdisciplinary faculty that includes:

  • Community organizers
  • Movement builders
  • Political scientists
  • Lawyers
  • Economists
  • Democracy experts
  • Governance practitioners

5. Alumni Network Access

Participants become part of a growing network of more than 400 alumni working across the democracy and development sectors throughout West Africa.

6. Practical Organizing Skills

The Institute emphasizes practical, field-tested approaches to community engagement and social change rather than academic theory.

2026 Program Schedule

The Regional Community Organizing Institute 2026 will take place in August 2026.

Applicants must be available to attend the entire duration of the training program. Successful candidates are expected to commit fully to all sessions and activities and should ensure that they can be away from their professional responsibilities during the training period.

Who Can Apply?

The Institute is designed specifically for practitioners actively engaged in advancing democracy, development, governance, and the rule of law.

Suitable applicants may include:

  • Leaders of non-profit organizations
  • Community advocates
  • Social campaigners
  • Policy reform advocates
  • Journalists
  • Political party leaders
  • Civic activists
  • Youth leaders
  • Student leaders
  • Community organizers
  • Democracy and governance practitioners

Applicants should currently occupy influential positions within their organizations, movements, campaigns, or communities and demonstrate a proven commitment to public service and social change.

Selection Criteria

Applicants must carefully review the following eligibility requirements before applying:

Age Requirement

Applicants must not be older than 30 years of age when the training begins in August 2026.

Residency Requirement

Applicants must be citizens of and currently residing in one of the following West African countries:

  • Benin Republic
  • Burkina Faso
  • Cape Verde
  • The Gambia
  • Ghana
  • Guinea
  • Guinea-Bissau
  • Ivory Coast
  • Liberia
  • Mali
  • Niger
  • Nigeria
  • Senegal
  • Sierra Leone
  • Togo

Professional Experience

Applicants must:

  • Be actively working in democracy, governance, development, civic engagement, or rule of law sectors.
  • Demonstrate practical experience and leadership.
  • Show evidence of professional and personal achievements in relevant fields.

Language Requirement

Applicants must possess a strong command of written and spoken:

  • English, or
  • French

All program activities and learning materials are delivered in English and French.

Inclusion and Diversity

Yiaga Africa strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women leaders
  • Female community organizers
  • Persons living with disabilities

Program Curriculum

The curriculum draws inspiration from Harvard Kennedy School’s renowned Leadership, Organizing and Action course and combines theoretical frameworks with practical organizing tools.

Participants will engage with topics including:

Political and Community Organizing

Understanding how to mobilize citizens, build grassroots movements, and strengthen civic participation.

Effective Policy Advocacy

Developing strategies for influencing public policy and driving meaningful reforms.

Non-Violent Change

Learning approaches for peaceful civic action and democratic engagement.

Scenario Building

Exploring techniques for strategic planning and future-focused decision-making.

Power Mapping

Identifying key stakeholders, decision-makers, and influence networks.

Coalition Building

Learning how to establish and sustain effective partnerships and collaborative networks.

Gender Mainstreaming

Integrating gender-responsive approaches into advocacy and organizational work.

Digital and Online Organizing

Using technology and digital tools to strengthen civic engagement and community mobilization.

Negotiation and Conflict Resolution

Developing negotiation skills for advocacy, partnerships, and leadership.

Transnational Organizing

Building regional alliances and strengthening cross-border collaboration.

Managing Effective Civic Organizations

Enhancing organizational leadership, sustainability, and impact.

Application Process

Applicants must submit all materials in either English or French.

The application consists of the following components:

  1. Applicant Information
  2. Essay Questions
  3. English Language Proficiency Assessment
  4. Video Submission
  5. Letters of Recommendation
  6. Personal Certification

Applicants should ensure that every section is completed accurately and thoroughly before submission.

Important Application Information

  • Applications remain open until July 14, 2026.
  • Early submission is strongly encouraged.
  • Selected applicants will be notified in July 2026.
  • Due to the large volume of applications, inquiries regarding application status before notification may not receive responses.
  • Incomplete applications may not be considered.

Final Thoughts

The Yiaga Africa Regional Community Organizing Institute 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for young leaders, civic actors, and democracy practitioners across West Africa to gain advanced organizing skills, strengthen their leadership capacity, and join a powerful regional network of change-makers.

For individuals committed to advancing democratic governance, community development, accountability, and social transformation, this fully funded program provides the knowledge, connections, and practical tools needed to create lasting impact within their communities and across the region.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Training Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Yiaga Africa Regional Community Organizing Institute 2026 for Young Democracy and Development Leaders in West Africa (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI Rapid Response Fellowship 2026: Earn $170K–$250K+ Working on AI Security with the U.S. Federal Government
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Horizon Institute for Public Service has launched applications for the AI Rapid Response Fellowship 2026, an exciting one-year opportunity designed to place experienced AI, cybersecurity, and policy professionals inside U.S. federal government offices to help address some of the nation’s most urgent artificial intelligence security challenges. As AI capabilities continue to evolve at an […]

The post AI Rapid Response Fellowship 2026: Earn $170K–$250K+ Working on AI Security with the U.S. Federal Government appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Horizon Institute for Public Service has launched applications for the AI Rapid Response Fellowship 2026, an exciting one-year opportunity designed to place experienced AI, cybersecurity, and policy professionals inside U.S. federal government offices to help address some of the nation’s most urgent artificial intelligence security challenges.

As AI capabilities continue to evolve at an unprecedented pace, governments around the world are racing to understand and manage the opportunities and risks associated with advanced AI systems. The United States has identified a major shortage of professionals with expertise in artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, national security, and related disciplines.

The AI Rapid Response Fellowship was created to bridge this talent gap by recruiting exceptional technical and policy experts who can immediately contribute to executive branch agencies working on AI security and national security priorities.

Application Deadline

22 July 2026

 

About the AI Rapid Response Fellowship

Unlike Horizon’s traditional fellowship designed for individuals transitioning into public service careers, the AI Rapid Response Fellowship specifically targets professionals with substantial prior experience who are ready to contribute from their very first day in government.

Successful fellows will work full-time within executive branch offices, supporting federal efforts to strengthen AI security, national security, cyber resilience, critical infrastructure protection, and emerging technology policy.

Most placements will be located in Washington, D.C., although a limited number of positions may be remote or based in other cities such as San Francisco where federal agencies maintain offices.

The fellowship lasts one year, with possible extensions depending on funding availability and host agency needs.

 

Why This Fellowship Matters

Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming science, healthcare, business, national defense, and economic development.

However, increasingly capable AI systems also introduce significant security concerns, including:

  • Cybersecurity threats
  • Critical infrastructure vulnerabilities
  • National security risks
  • AI misuse by foreign adversaries
  • Strategic competition with China
  • AI supply chain security
  • Frontier AI governance

Recent advancements in AI have demonstrated the growing ability of advanced models to identify software vulnerabilities, automate sophisticated cyber tasks, and influence national security.

To respond effectively, the U.S. government needs highly skilled professionals capable of navigating both the technical and policy dimensions of AI.

The AI Rapid Response Fellowship directly supports this mission.

 

Fellowship Tracks

Applicants may apply for either—or both—of the following tracks.

Fellow Track

Designed for professionals who possess significant technical or policy expertise related to AI security.

Typical experience:

  • 2–6+ years

Salary:

  • $170,000–$200,000+
  • Additional benefits stipend

 

Senior Fellow Track

Designed for highly experienced professionals capable of serving in senior federal leadership roles.

Typical experience:

  • 10–15+ years

Compensation:

  • Starting at $250,000+
  • Additional benefits stipend

Applicants unsure of the appropriate track may indicate interest in both during the application process.

 

Areas Fellows May Work On

Depending on the host office, fellows may contribute to projects involving:

  • Government adoption of AI for defense and intelligence
  • Critical infrastructure protection
  • Cybersecurity policy
  • AI model capability assessments
  • Vulnerability disclosure coordination
  • Protection of frontier AI developers
  • AI infrastructure security
  • AI supply chain resilience
  • AI research and development funding
  • Secure government AI computing systems
  • AI evaluation methodologies
  • AI robustness and control research
  • Monitoring global AI capabilities
  • Intelligence collection and analysis
  • AI national security strategy
  • Strategic technology competition
  • Public-private AI security collaboration

 

Federal Agencies and Offices

The Executive Order and National Security Presidential Memorandum identify numerous agencies expected to lead AI security efforts, including:

  • White House
  • Intelligence Community
  • Department of Treasury
  • Department of Defense
  • Department of Energy
  • Department of Homeland Security
  • Department of State
  • Department of Commerce

Successful fellows will be matched with offices based on their expertise and professional interests.

 

Benefits of the AI Rapid Response Fellowship

Selected fellows receive a comprehensive package that includes:

Competitive Compensation

  • Fellow Track: $170,000–$200,000+
  • Senior Fellow Track: Starting at $250,000+

Additional stipend for employee benefits.

 

Fully Funded Federal Placement

Participants work full-time within a U.S. federal office addressing national AI security priorities.

 

Professional Development

Fellows gain access to:

  • Briefings on frontier AI capabilities
  • AI policy training
  • Government process orientation
  • Career development support

 

Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of Horizon’s broader AI policy community, including:

  • Current fellows
  • Alumni
  • Government leaders
  • Industry experts
  • Academic researchers
  • Think tanks

 

Career Support

After completing the fellowship, participants continue to receive:

  • Career guidance
  • Professional networking
  • Connections to AI security organizations
  • Future leadership opportunities

 

Professional Development Budget

The fellowship includes funding for:

  • Conferences
  • Professional training
  • Career development activities

 

Relocation Assistance

Eligible fellows relocating to Washington, D.C., receive relocation support.

 

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship seeks professionals with expertise in fields such as:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Machine Learning
  • Cybersecurity
  • National Security
  • Intelligence
  • Biosecurity
  • Critical Infrastructure Protection
  • China Policy
  • AI Evaluations
  • Supply Chain Security
  • Technology Policy

Experience may come from:

  • Federal government
  • State government
  • Private sector
  • Technology companies
  • AI startups
  • Research institutions
  • Academia

Self-taught professionals with strong expertise are also encouraged to apply.

 

Minimum Requirements

Applicants should possess:

  • U.S. citizenship
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to write policy memos
  • Technical literacy
  • Subject matter expertise
  • Public service motivation

An existing security clearance is advantageous but not mandatory.

 

Fellowship Timeline

Application Deadline

22 July 2026

Selection Process

Applicants advancing through the process will complete:

  1. Interviews
  2. Short work assessment
  3. Reference checks

 

Offers

Initial fellowship offers are expected by:

Early to mid-September 2026

 

Matching Process

Selected finalists will meet with prospective federal host offices to identify the best placement.

Final participation is confirmed only after receiving a host office offer.

 

Earliest Start Date

Fellows are expected to begin as soon as administrative processing allows.

Applicants awaiting federal onboarding may temporarily work with nonprofit organizations engaged in AI security initiatives.

 

About Horizon Institute for Public Service

The Horizon Institute for Public Service is a nonpartisan nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening government capacity to address emerging technologies.

Through fellowships, leadership programs, workshops, career accelerators, and policy networks, Horizon helps develop the next generation of AI and emerging technology leaders serving the public interest.

The AI Rapid Response Fellowship builds upon Horizon’s flagship fellowship model with a focused initiative to rapidly strengthen federal AI security expertise.

 

Why You Should Apply

The AI Rapid Response Fellowship offers an extraordinary opportunity for experienced professionals to shape the future of AI governance and national security.

Participants will:

  • Influence national AI policy
  • Work alongside senior government officials
  • Address cutting-edge AI security challenges
  • Receive exceptional compensation
  • Expand professional networks
  • Build long-term careers in public service

If you have expertise in AI, cybersecurity, technology policy, or national security and are passionate about protecting the future of artificial intelligence, this fellowship represents one of the most impactful opportunities available in 2026.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post AI Rapid Response Fellowship 2026: Earn $170K–$250K+ Working on AI Security with the U.S. Federal Government appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Press Institute Transition Accelerator 2026: Up to €25,000 Funding for European Independent Media Organizations
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The International Press Institute (IPI) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Transition Accelerator, a comprehensive eight-month media innovation and digital transformation programme designed to help independent media organizations across Europe strengthen their sustainability, competitiveness, audience engagement, and revenue generation strategies. Following the success of previous editions, the Transition Accelerator continues to provide media organizations […]

The post International Press Institute Transition Accelerator 2026: Up to €25,000 Funding for European Independent Media Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Press Institute (IPI) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Transition Accelerator, a comprehensive eight-month media innovation and digital transformation programme designed to help independent media organizations across Europe strengthen their sustainability, competitiveness, audience engagement, and revenue generation strategies.

Following the success of previous editions, the Transition Accelerator continues to provide media organizations with funding, expert mentorship, coaching, training, networking opportunities, and practical tools to navigate the rapidly changing media landscape. As technological disruption, artificial intelligence, audience behavior shifts, and evolving business models continue to reshape journalism, this programme offers a unique opportunity for media leaders to build resilient and future-ready organizations.

About the International Press Institute Transition Accelerator

The International Press Institute Transition Accelerator is an intensive eight-month programme tailored for established, editorially independent media organizations seeking to develop innovative solutions that support long-term growth and sustainability.

The programme adopts an interdisciplinary approach, combining:

  • Audience engagement strategies
  • Revenue generation models
  • Product development
  • Business innovation
  • Management improvement
  • Technology adoption
  • Artificial intelligence integration
  • Community-centered journalism approaches

Participants receive practical support to identify key organizational challenges and implement solutions that improve performance, strengthen audience relationships, and increase financial sustainability.

The initiative is part of Media Innovation Europe and is co-funded by the European Union.

Why Apply for the International Press Institute Transition Accelerator?

The media industry is experiencing unprecedented transformation driven by technological innovation, changing consumer expectations, evolving revenue models, and the increasing role of artificial intelligence.

The Transition Accelerator is specifically designed for media leaders who are seeking answers to critical questions such as:

  • How can media organizations remain competitive in a rapidly changing digital environment?
  • What sustainable revenue models can support independent journalism?
  • How can organizations better understand and serve their audiences?
  • How can artificial intelligence be integrated responsibly into newsroom workflows?
  • What strategies can strengthen long-term organizational resilience?

Through the programme, participants gain access to industry-leading expertise and practical support that helps transform ideas into measurable outcomes.

Benefits of the Programme

Selected organizations will receive a wide range of benefits aimed at accelerating growth and innovation.

1. Funding of Up to €25,000

Each selected media organization may receive up to €25,000 to develop and implement a transformative project that addresses a key organizational challenge.

Projects may focus on:

  • Audience development
  • Revenue diversification
  • Product innovation
  • Workflow optimization
  • Organizational management
  • Artificial intelligence adoption
  • New journalism formats

2. In-Person Bootcamp in Vienna

Participants will attend a three-day intensive bootcamp in Vienna.

Key features include:

  • Attendance by 2–3 team members from each selected organization
  • Collaborative learning experiences
  • Expert-led sessions
  • Networking with peers and industry leaders
  • Practical innovation workshops

3. Organizational Needs Assessment

Each participating organization receives a comprehensive assessment covering:

  • Business maturity
  • Product development capacity
  • Audience engagement capabilities
  • Operational strengths and weaknesses

The assessment helps define a clear problem statement and ensures programme support is customized to organizational needs.

4. Weekly Workshops and Training

Participants gain access to regular workshops focused on practical skills and tools that can be applied long after the programme concludes.

Training areas include:

  • Media management
  • Audience engagement design
  • Product development
  • Revenue generation strategies
  • Performance measurement and analytics
  • Emerging journalism formats
  • Artificial intelligence implementation
  • Innovation management

5. Personalized Coaching and Advisory Services

Organizations benefit from one-on-one guidance from leading media experts.

This includes:

  • Tailored coaching sessions
  • Flexible expert consultations
  • Project-specific support
  • Strategic advice for growth and innovation

6. Peer Learning and Collaboration

Participants become part of a vibrant network of media innovators.

Benefits include:

  • Peer-to-peer learning
  • Group coaching sessions
  • Knowledge sharing opportunities
  • Collaborative problem-solving

7. Access to Exclusive Learning Resources

Selected participants receive access to:

  • Dedicated e-learning platforms
  • Recorded expert sessions
  • Training modules
  • Monthly Ask-Me-Anything sessions with industry specialists

8. Long-Term Professional Network

Organizations join a growing community consisting of:

  • Current cohort members
  • Programme alumni
  • Industry coaches
  • Media innovation leaders

This network provides valuable opportunities for future collaboration and professional growth.

What Types of Projects Are Supported?

The programme supports projects that help media organizations adapt and thrive in an evolving media environment.

Examples include:

  • Developing new revenue streams
  • Launching audience-focused products
  • Improving newsroom workflows
  • Enhancing organizational management systems
  • Introducing AI-powered solutions
  • Building community engagement initiatives
  • Creating innovative journalism formats
  • Improving audience retention strategies

Who Should Consider Applying?

The programme is designed for:

  • Independent investigative journalism organizations
  • Local newsrooms
  • Regional media outlets
  • National media organizations
  • Editorially independent media institutions

Organizations should demonstrate a commitment to innovation and possess a clear understanding of their audience and organizational goals.

Programme Structure

The Transition Accelerator combines several learning and development components, including:

  1. Organizational needs assessment
  2. Online learning modules
  3. Expert-led workshops
  4. Live training sessions
  5. In-person Vienna bootcamp
  6. Individual coaching
  7. Group coaching
  8. Peer exchange activities
  9. Access to industry experts
  10. Ongoing project development support

This comprehensive structure ensures participants receive both strategic guidance and practical implementation assistance.

Application Process

Interested organizations must complete and submit the official application form.

Important application information:

  • Applications can be saved and completed later.
  • Applicants receive an email containing a link to continue editing their application before submission.
  • Once submitted, applications cannot be edited.
  • Applications must be submitted in English.
  • Applications must be submitted through the designated Jotform platform.
  • Applicants will receive confirmation once their submission is successfully received.

Organizations uncertain about their eligibility are encouraged to contact the programme team or attend the upcoming information session.

Information Session

Prospective applicants can join an information session to learn more about the programme and ask questions directly.

Date: July 17, 2026

Time: 11:00 CEST

Application Deadline

Deadline: September 9, 2026 (23:59 CEST)

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

Interested organizations are encouraged to apply early and ensure all required information is completed before submission.

About the International Press Institute

The International Press Institute is a global network of editors, journalists, and media executives dedicated to defending media freedom and supporting independent journalism worldwide.

Through initiatives such as the Transition Accelerator, the organization helps media institutions develop innovative approaches that strengthen sustainability, editorial independence, and long-term impact in their respective markets.

For European media organizations seeking funding, expert support, innovation training, and strategic guidance, the International Press Institute Transition Accelerator 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity to accelerate transformation and secure a stronger future in an increasingly competitive media landscape.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post International Press Institute Transition Accelerator 2026: Up to €25,000 Funding for European Independent Media Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Iceland PhD Student in Economics Position 2026 in Reykjavík, Iceland (Fully Funded Research Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The University of Iceland is inviting applications for a full-time PhD Student in Economics position based in Reykjavík, Iceland. This exciting research opportunity offers aspiring economists the chance to contribute to groundbreaking work at the intersection of labor economics and welfare economics while working alongside leading national and international researchers. Funded through the University of […]

The post University of Iceland PhD Student in Economics Position 2026 in Reykjavík, Iceland (Fully Funded Research Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Iceland is inviting applications for a full-time PhD Student in Economics position based in Reykjavík, Iceland. This exciting research opportunity offers aspiring economists the chance to contribute to groundbreaking work at the intersection of labor economics and welfare economics while working alongside leading national and international researchers.

Funded through the University of Iceland Research Fund, this two-year PhD position is embedded within the Consortium on Compensating Income Variation (ConCIV), a renowned research team focused on advancing knowledge about the economic valuation of non-market outcomes and their impact on human well-being.

For candidates seeking an internationally recognized doctoral research experience, access to world-class academic supervision, and opportunities to publish in leading journals, this position represents an excellent pathway toward a successful research career.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 4 August 2026 (23:59 UTC)

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to ensure all required documents are properly prepared and uploaded.

About the University of Iceland

Founded in 1911, the University of Iceland has established itself as one of the country’s most influential academic institutions and a major contributor to Iceland’s social, economic, and scientific development.

Today, the university:

  • Enrolls approximately 14,000 students
  • Hosts more than 2,000 international students
  • Offers nearly 400 academic programs
  • Employs over 1,000 staff members
  • Participates actively in international scientific collaborations
  • Promotes diversity, inclusion, equality, and academic freedom

As Iceland’s largest university, it provides an international academic environment that attracts talented researchers and students from around the world.

Overview of the PhD Research Project

The successful PhD candidate will join the Consortium on Compensating Income Variation (ConCIV), a research group dedicated to understanding how non-market experiences affect individual well-being and how these effects can be translated into economic value.

The research project explores the economic value of major life events and public policy interventions, particularly focusing on:

  • Education
  • Retirement
  • Subjective well-being
  • Family welfare
  • Labor market outcomes
  • Public policy evaluation

Using the Compensating Income Variation (CIV) approach, the project aims to estimate the monetary value associated with educational attainment and retirement by examining their influence on the subjective well-being of individuals and their family members.

This innovative methodology provides policymakers with better tools for evaluating social programs and public investments.

Research Methodology

The PhD project will utilize longitudinal data from the Australian HILDA Survey (Household, Income and Labour Dynamics in Australia).

The candidate will conduct advanced quantitative analyses using extensive datasets to investigate important economic questions.

The research will make use of natural experiments, including:

  1. Changes in compulsory schooling laws
  2. Retirement age reforms
  3. Longitudinal well-being data
  4. Labor market information
  5. Household-level economic outcomes

These methods will help identify causal relationships and generate evidence-based conclusions regarding the value of education and retirement.

Supervision and Research Environment

The PhD student will be supervised by:

Dr. Tinna Laufey Ásgeirsdóttir, Professor of Economics at the University of Iceland.

The successful candidate will become part of a multidisciplinary research team consisting of both domestic and international experts. This collaborative environment provides opportunities for mentorship, networking, and participation in global research initiatives.

The project also benefits from the ConCIV group’s extensive international research collaborations, offering valuable exposure to leading scholars and institutions worldwide.

Main Responsibilities

The selected PhD student will be expected to undertake several important responsibilities throughout the appointment.

1. Research Participation

The candidate will actively participate in research activities alongside the principal supervisor and the broader research team.

2. Quantitative Data Analysis

Key duties include:

  • Working with large-scale datasets
  • Conducting advanced statistical analyses
  • Interpreting empirical findings
  • Contributing to research design and implementation

3. Scientific Publications

The doctoral research is expected to result in:

  • Scientific journal articles
  • Academic working papers
  • Conference papers
  • A doctoral dissertation

The dissertation will be based on at least three publishable papers intended for recognized international academic journals.

4. Knowledge Dissemination

The PhD student will also:

  • Present research at academic conferences
  • Participate in national and international scholarly communities
  • Engage in public outreach activities
  • Communicate findings to broader audiences

Benefits of the Position

Successful applicants will gain access to numerous academic and professional advantages, including:

  • Full-time funded PhD employment
  • Supervision from an experienced economics professor
  • Participation in an internationally connected research team
  • Opportunities to publish in recognized academic journals
  • Conference participation and networking opportunities
  • Exposure to advanced quantitative research methods
  • Experience working with internationally respected datasets
  • Residence in Reykjavík, Iceland
  • Access to a dynamic and inclusive university environment

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the position, applicants must satisfy the following criteria.

Academic Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A Master’s degree in Economics or a related discipline
  • Degree completion before the position commencement date

Technical Requirements

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Strong quantitative research skills
  • Experience with data analysis
  • Knowledge of empirical research methodologies
  • Ability to conduct independent research

Language Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Strong English language proficiency
  • Excellent written and verbal communication abilities

Personal Competencies

Successful candidates should possess:

  • Initiative
  • Independence
  • Organizational skills
  • Strong interpersonal abilities
  • Teamwork and collaboration skills

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following documents:

1. Cover Letter

A two-page cover letter in English explaining:

  • Interest in the research project
  • Relevant experience
  • Potential contributions to the project

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

A one- to two-page CV in English including:

  • Educational background
  • Work experience
  • Research experience
  • Relevant skills
  • Publications (if applicable)

3. Academic Documentation

Applicants must provide:

  • Certified degree certificates (Bachelor’s and Master’s)
  • Official academic transcripts

4. Research Work

Where applicable, applicants should submit:

  • Master’s thesis
  • Published research articles
  • Other relevant academic work

5. References

Applicants must provide:

  • Information for two referees
  • Contact details for each referee

Employment Conditions

Important employment conditions include:

  • Full-time appointment
  • Fixed-term contract of two years
  • Possibility of a reduced appointment percentage upon agreement with the principal supervisor
  • Appointment contingent upon admission to PhD studies at the Faculty of Economics at the University of Iceland

Applicants should note that acceptance into the PhD program is required before employment can be finalized.

Equality and Inclusion

The University of Iceland places significant emphasis on:

  • Equality
  • Diversity
  • Inclusion
  • Academic freedom
  • Professionalism

Recruitment decisions take the university’s equality policy into account, ensuring a fair and inclusive hiring process for all applicants.

Work Location

The position is based at:

University of Iceland
Reykjavík, Iceland

Working in Reykjavík offers researchers the opportunity to live in one of the world’s most innovative, safe, and environmentally conscious capital cities while participating in a vibrant international academic community.

APPLY HERE

With a unique research focus, strong international collaborations, advanced quantitative methodologies, and the opportunity to contribute to high-impact economic research, the University of Iceland PhD Student in Economics position offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers looking to advance their academic careers on a global stage.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here.

The post University of Iceland PhD Student in Economics Position 2026 in Reykjavík, Iceland (Fully Funded Research Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Oxford Quantitative Research Officer (Postdoctoral Researcher) Job 2026: Join the Young Lives Global Research Team in the United Kingdom
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The University of Oxford is inviting applications for the position of Quantitative Research Officer (Postdoctoral Researcher) within the renowned Young Lives research programme at the Oxford Department of International Development (ODID). This prestigious opportunity offers researchers the chance to contribute to internationally recognized research focused on child poverty, development, and social policy across multiple countries. […]

The post University of Oxford Quantitative Research Officer (Postdoctoral Researcher) Job 2026: Join the Young Lives Global Research Team in the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Oxford is inviting applications for the position of Quantitative Research Officer (Postdoctoral Researcher) within the renowned Young Lives research programme at the Oxford Department of International Development (ODID). This prestigious opportunity offers researchers the chance to contribute to internationally recognized research focused on child poverty, development, and social policy across multiple countries.

Based at Queen Elizabeth House, Oxford Department of International Development, the successful candidate will become part of a collaborative international research team that works across Oxford and the four Young Lives study countries. The role is ideal for researchers with strong quantitative skills who are passionate about generating evidence that informs policies aimed at improving the lives of children and young people in developing countries.

About the Young Lives Research Programme

Young Lives is one of the world’s leading longitudinal studies examining childhood poverty and development. The programme follows the lives of children and young people over an extended period, generating valuable evidence that helps policymakers, governments, development agencies, and researchers understand how poverty affects children’s lives and opportunities.

The research programme has built a strong reputation for producing high-quality evidence that influences development policy and contributes to global debates on education, poverty reduction, child wellbeing, social protection, and economic development.
